description: Comprehensive UI component patterns catalog for structural recognition and design system generation

patterns.name: Navigation Bar
patterns.description: Top-level horizontal navigation bar anchored to the top of the viewport, providing primary site-wide navigation, branding, and global actions.

variants.name: fixed
variants.when_to_use: When navigation must remain visible during scrolling

variants.name: transparent
variants.when_to_use: Hero sections where nav overlays content

variants.name: centered
variants.when_to_use: Marketing sites with symmetrical branding

variants.name: double_row
variants.when_to_use: Complex apps with primary and secondary nav tiers

accessibility_requirements: role='navigation' with aria-label describing the nav purpose
Landmark region so screen readers can jump to it
Skip-to-content link as first focusable element
Keyboard arrow navigation between top-level items
aria-current='page' on active link
Mobile menu toggle must have aria-expanded
responsive_behavior: Collapses to hamburger menu below tablet breakpoint
Logo may shrink or switch to icon-only on small screens
Action items may move into overflow menu
Search may collapse to icon trigger on mobile
common_mistakes: Missing skip-to-content link
Hamburger menu not trapping focus when open
Using div instead of nav element
Z-index wars with modals and drawers
Not announcing menu open/close state to screen readers
signals: nav element at top of DOM

patterns.name: Sidebar Navigation
patterns.description: Vertical navigation panel typically anchored to the left edge, providing hierarchical navigation for applications and dashboards.

variants.name: collapsible
variants.when_to_use: When users need to maximize content area

variants.name: icon_only
variants.when_to_use: Minimized state showing only icons

variants.name: nested
variants.when_to_use: Multi-level navigation hierarchies

variants.name: floating
variants.when_to_use: Overlay sidebar on mobile viewports

accessibility_requirements: role='navigation' with descriptive aria-label
aria-expanded on collapsible sections
Tree pattern (role='tree', role='treeitem') for nested nav
Keyboard up/down arrow navigation between items
Visual focus indicator on all interactive items
aria-current='page' on active item
responsive_behavior: Collapses to icon-only rail on medium screens
Becomes overlay drawer on mobile with backdrop
Touch-swipe to open/close on mobile
Content area adjusts width when sidebar toggles
common_mistakes: Sidebar pushes content off-screen instead of resizing
Nested items lack proper indentation cues
Active state not visible in collapsed mode
Missing keyboard navigation between sections
Scrollbar inside sidebar not styled or accessible

patterns.name: Breadcrumb
patterns.description: Horizontal trail showing the user's current location within the site hierarchy, enabling upward navigation to parent pages.

variants.name: simple
variants.when_to_use: Shallow hierarchies with 3-4 levels

variants.name: collapsed
variants.when_to_use: Deep hierarchies where middle items are hidden behind ellipsis

variants.name: with_icons
variants.when_to_use: When icons aid recognition of hierarchy levels

accessibility_requirements: nav element with aria-label='Breadcrumb'
Ordered list (ol) for semantic structure
aria-current='page' on the last item
Separators are decorative and hidden from screen readers via aria-hidden
Last item should not be a link
responsive_behavior: Truncate middle items with ellipsis on narrow screens
May show only parent and current page on mobile
Horizontal scroll with overflow hidden as fallback
common_mistakes: Making the current page a clickable link
Separators read aloud by screen readers
Using div instead of nav and ol elements
Not truncating on small screens causing horizontal overflow
Incorrect hierarchy order

patterns.name: Tabs
patterns.description: Horizontal or vertical tab interface for switching between related content panels within the same context without page navigation.

variants.name: underline
variants.when_to_use: Standard content tabs with bottom border indicator

variants.name: pill
variants.when_to_use: Contained tabs for segmented control appearance

variants.name: vertical
variants.when_to_use: Settings or configuration panels with many sections

variants.name: scrollable
variants.when_to_use: When tab count exceeds visible space

variants.name: closable
variants.when_to_use: Document or editor tabs that can be dismissed

accessibility_requirements: role='tablist' on container, role='tab' on each tab, role='tabpanel' on panels
aria-selected='true' on active tab
Arrow key navigation between tabs (left/right or up/down for vertical)
Tab key moves focus into the active panel, not to next tab
Each tab has aria-controls pointing to its panel id
Each panel has aria-labelledby pointing to its tab id
responsive_behavior: Overflow tabs become scrollable with arrow controls
May collapse into dropdown select on very narrow screens
Vertical tabs may stack into horizontal on mobile
Active indicator animation adapts to layout direction
common_mistakes: Tab key moves between tabs instead of into panel
Missing ARIA roles and relationships
All panels rendered and visible simultaneously
No visible focus indicator on tabs
Tabs wrapping to multiple rows instead of scrolling
signals: Content changes below without page load

patterns.name: Pagination
patterns.description: Page-based navigation control for browsing through large datasets or content collections divided into discrete pages.

variants.name: numbered
variants.when_to_use: When users need to jump to specific pages

variants.name: simple
variants.when_to_use: Previous/next only for sequential browsing

variants.name: cursor_based
variants.when_to_use: Infinite datasets without known total count

variants.name: load_more
variants.when_to_use: Appending content without page breaks

accessibility_requirements: nav element with aria-label='Pagination'
aria-current='page' on active page number
Previous/next buttons disabled (not hidden) at bounds
aria-label on each page button (e.g., 'Go to page 3')
Announce page changes to screen readers via live region
responsive_behavior: Reduce visible page numbers on smaller screens
Show only prev/next with current page indicator on mobile
Page size selector may hide on mobile
Center alignment or full-width on narrow viewports
common_mistakes: Hiding instead of disabling prev/next at boundaries
No indication of current page
Not preserving scroll position on page change
Missing keyboard support for page number buttons
Loading state not communicated to assistive tech
signals: Located at bottom of lists or tables

patterns.name: Command Palette
patterns.description: Keyboard-driven search overlay (typically triggered by Cmd+K or Ctrl+K) enabling rapid navigation, action execution, and content search.

variants.name: search_only
variants.when_to_use: Pure navigation search without command execution

variants.name: with_commands
variants.when_to_use: Combined search and action palette like VS Code

variants.name: nested
variants.when_to_use: Drill-down sub-menus for complex action trees

variants.name: contextual
variants.when_to_use: Results filtered by current application context

accessibility_requirements: role='combobox' on search input with aria-expanded
role='listbox' on results list
aria-activedescendant for virtual focus on results
Escape key closes palette and returns focus to trigger
Keyboard shortcut announced or discoverable
Results list uses role='option' for each item
responsive_behavior: Full-width on mobile with no backdrop margins
Results list may have reduced max-height on small screens
Touch-friendly result item sizing on mobile
May replace standard search on mobile
common_mistakes: Not trapping focus within the palette when open
Search not debounced causing performance issues
Keyboard shortcut conflicting with browser or OS shortcuts
Results not keyboard-navigable
Not restoring focus after palette closes

patterns.name: Bottom Navigation
patterns.description: Fixed bottom bar with 3-5 primary navigation destinations, standard pattern for mobile-first applications.

variants.name: labeled
variants.when_to_use: Standard bottom nav with icon and text label

variants.name: shifting
variants.when_to_use: Active item expands while others shrink (Material Design)

variants.name: fab_integrated
variants.when_to_use: Center item is a raised floating action button

variants.name: hidden_on_scroll
variants.when_to_use: Hides on scroll down, reappears on scroll up

accessibility_requirements: role='navigation' with aria-label
aria-current='page' on active destination
Labels always visible, not icon-only
Minimum 48x48dp touch targets
Badge count announced via aria-label on the nav item
responsive_behavior: Only shown on mobile viewports, replaced by sidebar or navbar on desktop
Safe area inset padding on notched devices
Landscape orientation may reduce icon size
common_mistakes: More than 5 items causing crowding
Icon-only without labels reducing discoverability
Not accounting for safe area insets on modern phones
Active state not distinct enough from inactive
Navigation items triggering on touch-down instead of touch-up
signals: Fixed bar at bottom of viewport

patterns.name: Mega Menu
patterns.description: Large dropdown panel triggered from the top navigation, displaying categorized links, featured content, and promotional areas across multiple columns.

variants.name: full_width
variants.when_to_use: Spans the entire viewport width for content-rich sites

variants.name: contained
variants.when_to_use: Fixed-width dropdown aligned to trigger

variants.name: tabbed
variants.when_to_use: Top-level categories as tabs within the mega menu

variants.name: with_media
variants.when_to_use: Includes images or promotional content alongside links

accessibility_requirements: Trigger uses aria-expanded and aria-haspopup='true'
Menu content accessible via keyboard Tab and arrow keys
Escape closes menu and returns focus to trigger
Hover intent delay prevents accidental open/close
Screen reader announces menu open state
Links organized in semantic groups with headings
responsive_behavior: Converts to accordion or nested list on mobile
Full-width panel may become scrollable column on tablet
Featured content may be hidden on small screens
Touch-friendly with explicit open/close instead of hover
common_mistakes: Opens on hover with no delay causing flicker
Diagonal mouse movement closes menu (missing hover intent triangles)
Not keyboard navigable
Panel obscures important content without dismiss option
Too many links overwhelming users

patterns.name: Stepper
patterns.description: Multi-step process navigation showing progress through a sequential workflow, such as checkout flows, onboarding, or form wizards.

variants.name: horizontal
variants.when_to_use: Standard left-to-right step progression

variants.name: vertical
variants.when_to_use: Steps with substantial inline content

variants.name: non_linear
variants.when_to_use: Users can jump to any completed or available step

variants.name: compact
variants.when_to_use: Limited space showing only current step with count

accessibility_requirements: aria-current='step' on the active step
Each step has aria-label describing its status (e.g., 'Step 2 of 4: Shipping, completed')
Navigation buttons clearly labeled (not just 'Next')
Error states on steps announced to screen readers
Progress communicated via aria-live region on step change
responsive_behavior: Horizontal stepper may collapse to show only current step on mobile
Step labels may truncate or hide on narrow screens
Vertical stepper works well on all screen sizes
Navigation buttons stack vertically on mobile
common_mistakes: Allowing forward navigation to steps with incomplete prerequisites
No way to return to previous steps
Step count not visible making users uncertain of remaining work
Losing form data when navigating between steps
Not validating current step before allowing progression

patterns.name: Text Input
patterns.description: Single-line text field for capturing short-form user input such as names, emails, or search queries.

variants.name: outlined
variants.when_to_use: Standard bordered input for most form contexts

variants.name: filled
variants.when_to_use: Filled background style for denser forms

variants.name: underlined
variants.when_to_use: Minimal style with only bottom border

variants.name: with_addon
variants.when_to_use: Prefix or suffix text/icons for formatted input (e.g., currency)

accessibility_requirements: Visible label associated via for/id or aria-labelledby
aria-describedby linking to helper text and error messages
aria-invalid='true' when in error state
aria-required='true' for required fields
Error messages announced via aria-live='polite'
Sufficient color contrast for placeholder text
responsive_behavior: Full-width on mobile viewports
Appropriate input type triggers correct mobile keyboard (email, tel, url)
Font size >= 16px to prevent iOS zoom on focus
Touch target meets minimum 44x44 points
common_mistakes: Placeholder used as the only label
Error message not associated with input via aria-describedby
Label disappears on focus (floating label without fallback)
Input too small for expected content
Not using appropriate input type for data format

patterns.name: Textarea
patterns.description: Multi-line text field for capturing longer-form content such as comments, descriptions, or messages.

variants.name: fixed_height
variants.when_to_use: Predetermined height for consistent layouts

variants.name: auto_grow
variants.when_to_use: Expands vertically as content increases

variants.name: with_toolbar
variants.when_to_use: Basic formatting options above the field

variants.name: with_character_limit
variants.when_to_use: When input length must be constrained

accessibility_requirements: Visible label associated via for/id
aria-describedby for helper text and character count
aria-invalid='true' in error state
Resize handle must be keyboard operable or auto-grow used
Character count announced periodically, not on every keystroke
responsive_behavior: Full-width on mobile viewports
Min-height ensures usable touch target
Auto-grow prevents need for scrolling within field
Font size >= 16px to prevent iOS zoom
common_mistakes: Too small default height making content invisible
Resize disabled without auto-grow alternative
Character count not accessible to screen readers
No max-height causing page layout disruption with auto-grow
Horizontal resize allowed causing layout breaks

patterns.name: Select
patterns.description: Dropdown selection control allowing users to choose one option from a predefined list.

variants.name: native
variants.when_to_use: Simple selections where native OS dropdown is acceptable

variants.name: custom
variants.when_to_use: When options need custom rendering (icons, descriptions)

variants.name: searchable
variants.when_to_use: Long option lists that benefit from filtering

variants.name: multi_select
variants.when_to_use: When multiple options can be selected simultaneously

variants.name: grouped
variants.when_to_use: Options organized into logical categories

accessibility_requirements: role='combobox' for custom selects with aria-expanded
role='listbox' on the options list, role='option' on items
Arrow key navigation through options
Type-ahead to jump to matching options
Selected option announced by screen reader
Label associated via aria-labelledby
responsive_behavior: Native select may be preferred on mobile for OS picker
Custom dropdown positions above if near viewport bottom
Full-width trigger on mobile
Options list may become scrollable with max-height
common_mistakes: Custom select not keyboard navigable
Options list renders off-screen
No type-ahead search in long lists
Using select for fewer than 5 options (radio group is better)
Placeholder option ('Select...') submitted as valid value
signals: Options list appears below on interaction
select element or custom combobox pattern

patterns.name: Checkbox
patterns.description: Boolean toggle for selecting one or more independent options from a set, or toggling a single option on/off.

variants.name: single
variants.when_to_use: Standalone boolean toggle like terms acceptance

variants.name: group
variants.when_to_use: Multiple related options that can be independently selected

variants.name: with_description
variants.when_to_use: Options that need additional explanation text

variants.name: parent_child
variants.when_to_use: Hierarchical select-all with indeterminate state

accessibility_requirements: role='checkbox' with aria-checked (true, false, or mixed)
Label clickable and associated with input
Space key toggles the checkbox
Group wrapped in fieldset with legend
Indeterminate state communicated via aria-checked='mixed'
Error state linked via aria-describedby
responsive_behavior: Touch target minimum 44x44 points
Label text wraps, checkbox stays aligned to first line
Group may switch from horizontal to vertical on mobile
common_mistakes: Checkbox without associated label
Indeterminate state not communicated to assistive tech
Group without fieldset/legend context
Visual-only checkbox without proper input element
Using checkboxes for mutually exclusive options (use radio)

patterns.name: Radio Group
patterns.description: Set of mutually exclusive options where exactly one option must be selected from the group.

variants.name: vertical_list
variants.when_to_use: Standard vertical stack for 3-7 options

variants.name: horizontal
variants.when_to_use: Few short options that fit in a row

variants.name: card_select
variants.when_to_use: Rich options with icons and descriptions

variants.name: button_group
variants.when_to_use: Segmented control appearance for 2-4 options

accessibility_requirements: Wrapped in fieldset with legend describing the group
role='radiogroup' if not using fieldset
Arrow keys move selection between options
Tab moves focus into/out of the group, not between items
Only the selected radio (or first if none selected) is in tab order
aria-required on the group if selection is mandatory
responsive_behavior: Horizontal groups stack vertically on mobile
Card-style options may shift to single column
Touch targets meet minimum size requirements
common_mistakes: Tab key moves between radios instead of arrow keys
No default selection when one is required
Missing fieldset/legend grouping
Using radios for multi-select (use checkboxes)
All radios in tab order instead of roving tabindex

patterns.name: Toggle Switch
patterns.description: Binary on/off control that takes effect immediately without requiring form submission, commonly used for settings and preferences.

variants.name: simple
variants.when_to_use: Standard toggle without text indicators

variants.name: with_labels
variants.when_to_use: On/Off text shown on track for clarity

variants.name: with_icon
variants.when_to_use: Icons (check/x) on thumb for visual reinforcement

variants.name: color_coded
variants.when_to_use: Different track colors for on/off states

accessibility_requirements: role='switch' with aria-checked
Associated visible label describing what is toggled
Space or Enter key toggles the switch
State change announced by screen reader
Not relying solely on color to indicate state
responsive_behavior: Touch target meets minimum 44x44 points
Label and switch may reflow on narrow screens
Thumb size remains consistent across viewports
common_mistakes: Using toggle for actions that need form submission (use checkbox)
No visible label, only relying on context
State not immediately obvious (which side is on?)
Using role='checkbox' instead of role='switch'
Animation interferes with reduced-motion preferences

patterns.name: Slider
patterns.description: Range input control allowing users to select a value or range within a minimum and maximum by dragging a thumb along a track.

variants.name: single
variants.when_to_use: Selecting one value from a range

variants.name: range
variants.when_to_use: Selecting a min-max range with two thumbs

variants.name: with_input
variants.when_to_use: Paired with number input for precise value entry

variants.name: stepped
variants.when_to_use: Discrete values with visible tick marks

accessibility_requirements: role='slider' with aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow
aria-valuetext for human-readable value (e.g., '$50' not '50')
Arrow keys adjust value by step increment
Page up/down for larger increments
Home/End keys jump to min/max
Visible label associated via aria-labelledby
responsive_behavior: Full-width track on mobile for easier thumb targeting
Value tooltip may show on touch instead of hover
Paired number input may stack below on mobile
Thumb size increases for touch interaction
common_mistakes: Thumb too small for touch interaction
No way to enter precise value (pair with input)
Missing aria-valuetext for non-numeric semantics
Step increments too fine for the use case
Track click doesn't move thumb to clicked position

patterns.name: Date Picker
patterns.description: Calendar-based date selection control supporting single date, date range, and various date format requirements.

variants.name: single
variants.when_to_use: Selecting one specific date

variants.name: range
variants.when_to_use: Selecting a start and end date

variants.name: datetime
variants.when_to_use: Date combined with time selection

variants.name: inline
variants.when_to_use: Calendar always visible, not in a popup

variants.name: month_year
variants.when_to_use: Selecting only month and year without specific day

accessibility_requirements: Grid pattern with role='grid' for the calendar
Arrow keys navigate between days
Page up/down navigate between months
aria-selected='true' on chosen date
Disabled dates use aria-disabled='true'
Live region announces month changes
Input accepts typed date format as alternative to calendar
responsive_behavior: Calendar popup may go full-width on mobile
Touch-friendly day cells with adequate sizing
May use native date picker on mobile as fallback
Range picker may show single month instead of two on mobile
common_mistakes: No keyboard navigation in calendar grid
Date format not matching user's locale
No text input alternative to calendar interaction
Disabled dates not visually or programmatically distinct
Calendar popup not repositioned to stay in viewport
signals: Input field that opens calendar popup

patterns.name: File Upload
patterns.description: Input control for selecting and uploading files from the user's device, supporting drag-and-drop, multiple files, and upload progress.

variants.name: button
variants.when_to_use: Simple file selection via button click

variants.name: dropzone
variants.when_to_use: Large drop area for drag-and-drop workflows

variants.name: avatar
variants.when_to_use: Single image upload with preview (profile pictures)

variants.name: multi
variants.when_to_use: Multiple file upload with file list management

accessibility_requirements: File input accessible via keyboard
Drop zone has role='button' and keyboard activation
Upload progress announced via aria-live region
File type restrictions communicated in helper text
Error messages associated with specific files
Remove button for each file has descriptive aria-label
responsive_behavior: Drop zone may use camera/gallery on mobile devices
File list stacks vertically on narrow screens
Progress indicators remain visible during upload
Drop zone sizing adapts to container
common_mistakes: No feedback during upload progress
File type restrictions not communicated before selection
No way to remove files before submission
Drag-and-drop only with no button fallback
Large file uploads without chunking or resumability

patterns.name: Autocomplete
patterns.description: Text input with search-as-you-type suggestion dropdown, enabling efficient selection from large datasets or API-driven results.

variants.name: single
variants.when_to_use: Select one value from suggestions

variants.name: multi
variants.when_to_use: Select multiple values shown as tags/chips

variants.name: freesolo
variants.when_to_use: Accept typed value even if not in suggestions

variants.name: grouped
variants.when_to_use: Suggestions organized by category

accessibility_requirements: role='combobox' with aria-expanded, aria-autocomplete
role='listbox' on suggestions, role='option' on items
aria-activedescendant for virtual focus management
Arrow keys navigate suggestions, Enter selects
Escape clears suggestions and restores input value
Screen reader announces suggestion count
responsive_behavior: Suggestions list positioned to stay within viewport
Full-width suggestions panel on mobile
Virtual keyboard accounted for in suggestion positioning
Touch-friendly suggestion item height
common_mistakes: Suggestions not keyboard navigable
No debounce on API calls during typing
Suggestions cover the input field on mobile
Not clearing suggestions when input is emptied
Race conditions between fast typing and slow API responses

patterns.name: Form Validation
patterns.description: System pattern for communicating field-level and form-level validation states including errors, warnings, and success confirmations.

variants.name: inline
variants.when_to_use: Error messages directly below each field

variants.name: summary
variants.when_to_use: Error list at top of form with anchor links to fields

variants.name: real_time
variants.when_to_use: Validation on blur or after initial submit attempt

variants.name: progressive
variants.when_to_use: Only validate after user interacts with field

accessibility_requirements: aria-invalid='true' on invalid fields
Error messages linked via aria-describedby
Error summary uses role='alert' or aria-live='assertive'
Focus moves to first error on form submission failure
Error icons have aria-hidden with text alternatives
Required fields marked with aria-required='true'
responsive_behavior: Error messages don't cause layout shifts that push content off-screen
Error summary scrolls to top on mobile submission
Inline errors visible without scrolling on mobile
common_mistakes: Validating on every keystroke (validate on blur instead)
Error messages not associated with fields programmatically
Only using color to indicate errors (add icon and text)
Not moving focus to first error after submission
Clearing all field values on submission failure
Displaying errors before user has finished typing

patterns.name: Search Input
patterns.description: Specialized text input for search functionality with clear button, submit action, and optional inline results.

variants.name: simple
variants.when_to_use: Basic search input with icon

variants.name: expandable
variants.when_to_use: Collapsed icon that expands to input on click

variants.name: with_filters
variants.when_to_use: Search combined with filter dropdowns

variants.name: with_suggestions
variants.when_to_use: Autocomplete suggestions as user types

accessibility_requirements: role='search' on containing form or div
aria-label='Search' if no visible label
Clear button has aria-label='Clear search'
Submit via Enter key
Loading state announced via aria-live
Search results count announced to screen readers
responsive_behavior: May collapse to icon-only on narrow screens
Full-width on mobile when expanded
Filters may move to separate filter panel on mobile
common_mistakes: No clear button when field has content
Missing search icon making input purpose unclear
No loading feedback during search
Not using input type='search' for semantic meaning
Submit button hidden or missing

patterns.name: Number Input
patterns.description: Numeric input field with increment/decrement controls for entering precise numerical values within defined bounds.

variants.name: with_stepper
variants.when_to_use: Plus/minus buttons for easy adjustment

variants.name: plain
variants.when_to_use: Simple numeric input without stepper buttons

variants.name: with_unit
variants.when_to_use: Input with unit suffix (kg, px, %)

variants.name: currency
variants.when_to_use: Formatted currency input with symbol prefix

accessibility_requirements: role='spinbutton' with aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow
Arrow up/down to increment/decrement
Buttons labeled with aria-label ('Increase quantity', 'Decrease quantity')
Disabled increment at max, decrement at min
Input accepts direct keyboard entry
responsive_behavior: Uses inputmode='numeric' for mobile number keyboard
Stepper buttons sized for touch targets
Full-width or fixed-width depending on value range
common_mistakes: Accepting non-numeric characters without feedback
No bounds enforcement on direct keyboard entry
Stepper buttons too small for touch
Not formatting large numbers with separators
Scrolling page while trying to use arrow keys in field

patterns.name: Color Picker
patterns.description: Interactive color selection control supporting various color models (HEX, RGB, HSL) with visual preview and precision input.

variants.name: swatch_only
variants.when_to_use: Simple selection from predefined palette

variants.name: full
variants.when_to_use: Complete picker with gradient area and sliders

variants.name: input_only
variants.when_to_use: Text input for HEX/RGB value entry

variants.name: with_presets
variants.when_to_use: Combination of preset swatches and custom picker

accessibility_requirements: Trigger swatch shows current color with text label
Gradient area navigable via arrow keys
Slider accessible as range input
Color value announced to screen readers
Preset swatches have descriptive labels (not just 'Red')
Text input alternative for precise values
responsive_behavior: Popup may go full-width on mobile
Gradient area resizes proportionally
Preset swatches wrap to available width
Touch-friendly slider thumbs
common_mistakes: No text input alternative for accessibility
Gradient area not keyboard navigable
Color names not provided alongside values
Preset colors not sufficient for common needs
Alpha channel ignored when needed

patterns.name: Rich Text Editor
patterns.description: WYSIWYG formatted text editing component supporting bold, italic, lists, links, images, and other structured content.

variants.name: minimal
variants.when_to_use: Basic formatting: bold, italic, links, lists

variants.name: standard
variants.when_to_use: Common formatting plus headings, images, tables

variants.name: full
variants.when_to_use: Complete feature set including code blocks, embeds

variants.name: markdown
variants.when_to_use: Markdown input with live preview

accessibility_requirements: Toolbar buttons have aria-label and aria-pressed for toggle states
Content area has role='textbox' with aria-multiline='true'
Keyboard shortcuts for all formatting actions (Ctrl+B, etc.)
Focus management between toolbar and content area
Content structure uses semantic HTML (h1-h6, ul, ol)
Screen reader announces formatting changes
responsive_behavior: Toolbar wraps or becomes scrollable on narrow screens
Less common formatting options hidden behind overflow menu
Image insertion uses mobile camera/gallery
Content area minimum height ensures usability
common_mistakes: Outputting insecure HTML (XSS vulnerability)
Toolbar buttons without accessible labels
Pasting content that breaks layout
No sanitization of pasted HTML
Content area too small for meaningful editing

patterns.name: Data Table
patterns.description: Structured data grid supporting sorting, filtering, pagination, row selection, and column customization for tabular data presentation.

variants.name: simple
variants.when_to_use: Static data display without interaction

variants.name: sortable
variants.when_to_use: Columns can be sorted ascending/descending

variants.name: selectable
variants.when_to_use: Rows can be selected for bulk actions

variants.name: expandable
variants.when_to_use: Rows expand to show additional detail

variants.name: editable
variants.when_to_use: Inline cell editing for data management

variants.name: virtualized
variants.when_to_use: Large datasets with virtualized scrolling

accessibility_requirements: Proper table semantics (table, thead, tbody, th, td)
Column headers use scope='col'
Sort state communicated via aria-sort on th
Row selection uses aria-selected
Caption or aria-label describes the table
Keyboard navigation through cells (arrow keys optional but preferred)
Selection checkbox has descriptive label per row
responsive_behavior: Horizontal scroll with sticky first column on narrow screens
Column hiding by priority on smaller viewports
Card layout transformation on mobile
Stacked layout where each cell is labeled on mobile
common_mistakes: Using div layout instead of table elements
Horizontal overflow without scroll indication
Sort state not communicated to screen readers
No loading or empty states
Fixed column widths causing text truncation
Missing caption or summary for table purpose

patterns.name: List
patterns.description: Vertical arrangement of related items, each containing primary text, optional secondary text, icons, and actions.

variants.name: simple
variants.when_to_use: Text-only list items

variants.name: two_line
variants.when_to_use: Items with primary and secondary text

variants.name: interactive
variants.when_to_use: Clickable items that navigate or trigger actions

variants.name: with_avatars
variants.when_to_use: User or entity lists with visual identifiers

variants.name: ordered
variants.when_to_use: Numbered sequential items

accessibility_requirements: Semantic list element (ul, ol, or role='list')
List items use role='listitem'
Interactive items are keyboard focusable
Selection state uses aria-selected or aria-current
Group label via aria-label on list container
Action buttons within items have descriptive labels
responsive_behavior: Full-width items on mobile
Trailing actions may collapse into overflow menu
Leading icons may resize on smaller screens
Two-line items may become three-line on narrow screens
common_mistakes: Not using semantic list elements
Clickable items without keyboard support
Dividers included for last item
Inconsistent item heights in same list
Too many actions per item creating clutter
signals: ul or ol element in DOM

patterns.name: Card
patterns.description: Self-contained content block with optional media, text, and actions, serving as an entry point to more detailed information.

variants.name: basic
variants.when_to_use: Simple content container without media

variants.name: media
variants.when_to_use: Card with image or video header

variants.name: horizontal
variants.when_to_use: Media on the side for wider layouts

variants.name: interactive
variants.when_to_use: Entire card is clickable as a link

variants.name: elevated
variants.when_to_use: Shadow-based visual separation

variants.name: outlined
variants.when_to_use: Border-based visual separation

accessibility_requirements: Card title should be a heading element for structure
Interactive cards use a single anchor or button wrapping content
Action buttons have descriptive labels (not just 'Learn more')
Images use meaningful alt text
Clickable cards should not nest interactive elements
Focus indicator visible on the interactive boundary
responsive_behavior: Cards reflow from multi-column to single column on mobile
Horizontal cards may stack media above content on mobile
Card width adapts to grid column or container
Image aspect ratio maintained during resize
common_mistakes: Nesting links/buttons inside clickable card (nested interactives)
Image without alt text
Inconsistent card heights in a grid
No truncation strategy for variable-length content
Card actions with generic labels like 'Click here'

patterns.name: Avatar
patterns.description: Visual representation of a user or entity, displaying a photo, initials, or fallback icon in a circular or rounded container.

variants.name: image
variants.when_to_use: User has uploaded a profile photo

variants.name: initials
variants.when_to_use: No photo available, show first/last initials

variants.name: icon
variants.when_to_use: Generic user placeholder icon

variants.name: group
variants.when_to_use: Overlapping stack showing multiple users

variants.name: with_status
variants.when_to_use: Online/offline/away status indicator dot

accessibility_requirements: Image avatar has alt text with user's name
Initials have aria-label with full name
Status indicator has screen reader text (e.g., 'Online')
Group avatar announces count (e.g., '5 team members')
Decorative avatars use aria-hidden='true'
responsive_behavior: Size scales across predefined size tokens (xs, sm, md, lg, xl)
Group avatar overlap adjusts with avatar size
Status dot scales proportionally
common_mistakes: No fallback when image fails to load
Initials not contrasted against background color
Status indicator too small to perceive
Group avatars without count indicator for overflow
Alt text says 'avatar' instead of user's name
signals: Circular image or colored circle with letters

patterns.name: Badge
patterns.description: Small overlaid indicator showing count, status, or attention marker on another element such as an icon, avatar, or button.

variants.name: count
variants.when_to_use: Numeric count (notifications, items)

variants.name: dot
variants.when_to_use: Simple attention indicator without count

variants.name: status
variants.when_to_use: Colored indicator for online/error/warning states

variants.name: label
variants.when_to_use: Short text label (e.g., 'New', 'Beta')

accessibility_requirements: Count announced via aria-label on parent (e.g., 'Notifications, 5 unread')
Dot badge has screen reader text describing its meaning
Badge content updates trigger aria-live announcement
Visual badge not the sole method of communication
responsive_behavior: Badge size and position remain consistent across viewports
Overflow value (99+) used for large counts
Dot variant used when space is extremely constrained
common_mistakes: Badge hidden behind adjacent elements due to z-index
No maximum count cap (showing '10432' in tiny badge)
Count not accessible to screen readers
Badge position inconsistent across anchor element sizes
Using badge color alone to convey meaning
signals: Small circle overlaid on corner of element

patterns.name: Tag
patterns.description: Compact label element for categorization, filtering, or metadata display, often displayed in groups and optionally dismissible.

variants.name: static
variants.when_to_use: Display-only categorization labels

variants.name: removable
variants.when_to_use: User can dismiss/remove the tag

variants.name: selectable
variants.when_to_use: Tags act as filter toggles

variants.name: with_icon
variants.when_to_use: Icon reinforces the tag category

variants.name: colored
variants.when_to_use: Category-specific color coding

accessibility_requirements: Remove button has aria-label (e.g., 'Remove tag: JavaScript')
Selectable tags use aria-pressed or aria-selected
Tag group wrapped with descriptive label
Remove action not focus-trapping (focus moves logically)
Color not the only differentiator between tag types
responsive_behavior: Tags wrap to new lines within container
Touch targets meet minimum 44px for interactive tags
Tag text truncates with ellipsis if needed
Group may horizontally scroll on mobile
common_mistakes: Remove button too small to tap on mobile
Relying only on color to distinguish categories
Tags not wrapping, causing horizontal overflow
No keyboard support for selectable tags
Inconsistent tag sizes within same group

patterns.name: Tooltip
patterns.description: Small informational text overlay that appears on hover or focus to describe an element, providing supplementary but non-essential information.

variants.name: simple
variants.when_to_use: Plain text description of an element

variants.name: rich
variants.when_to_use: Formatted content with links or actions (use popover instead)

variants.name: positioned
variants.when_to_use: Explicit placement (top, right, bottom, left)

accessibility_requirements: Tooltip content set via aria-describedby on trigger
Appears on keyboard focus, not just mouse hover
Escape key dismisses tooltip
role='tooltip' on tooltip element
Tooltip remains visible while hovered (WCAG 1.4.13)
Not used for essential information (use visible text instead)
responsive_behavior: Reposition to stay within viewport
May not appear on touch devices (use alternatives)
Arrow points to trigger regardless of position
Consider alternatives on mobile where hover doesn't exist
common_mistakes: Used for essential information not available elsewhere
Not appearing on keyboard focus
Disappears when trying to hover the tooltip itself
Content too long for tooltip format
Not repositioning when clipped by viewport
Used on touch devices where hover is unreliable
signals: Small dark box near element on hover

patterns.name: Popover
patterns.description: Click-triggered floating overlay containing interactive content such as forms, menus, or detailed information, positioned relative to a trigger element.

variants.name: informational
variants.when_to_use: Displaying detailed information on demand

variants.name: interactive
variants.when_to_use: Contains form elements or actions

variants.name: confirmation
variants.when_to_use: Inline confirmation before destructive action

variants.name: menu
variants.when_to_use: Action list triggered from element

accessibility_requirements: Trigger has aria-expanded and aria-haspopup
Focus moves into popover when opened
Escape closes popover and returns focus to trigger
Focus trapped within popover while open
role='dialog' for interactive popovers
Close button available within popover
responsive_behavior: Repositions to stay within viewport
May become full-width sheet on mobile
Arrow adjusts position to maintain trigger alignment
Touch outside closes popover on mobile
common_mistakes: Not trapping focus within interactive popovers
Multiple popovers open simultaneously
Popover content inaccessible via keyboard
Not closing on outside click
Popover renders off-screen without repositioning

patterns.name: Stat Card
patterns.description: Dashboard metric display showing a key value with optional label, trend indicator, icon, and comparison context.

variants.name: simple
variants.when_to_use: Value and label only

variants.name: with_trend
variants.when_to_use: Shows change direction and percentage

variants.name: with_sparkline
variants.when_to_use: Mini chart showing recent trend

variants.name: with_comparison
variants.when_to_use: Compared against previous period or target

accessibility_requirements: Value and label create meaningful combined text
Trend direction communicated via text, not just color/icon
Sparkline has aria-label summarizing the trend
Use appropriate heading level for metric label
Comparison context included in accessible description
responsive_behavior: Cards reflow from horizontal row to grid on mobile
Value font size may scale down slightly on mobile
Sparkline may hide on very small viewports
Grid typically 4-across on desktop, 2-across on tablet, stacked on mobile
common_mistakes: Trend color as only indicator (red/green without icon/text)
No loading state causing layout shift when data arrives
Metric value without context (what does '432' mean?)
Inconsistent card heights in a row
Sparkline without accessible description
signals: Small label below or above number

patterns.name: Timeline
patterns.description: Chronological display of events or activities along a vertical or horizontal axis with timestamps, descriptions, and status indicators.

variants.name: vertical
variants.when_to_use: Standard chronological feed of events

variants.name: horizontal
variants.when_to_use: Process or milestone visualization

variants.name: alternating
variants.when_to_use: Events alternate left/right of axis

variants.name: compact
variants.when_to_use: Condensed activity log format

accessibility_requirements: Ordered list semantics (ol) for chronological order
Timestamps use time element with datetime attribute
Status icons have text alternatives
Visual connectors are decorative (aria-hidden)
Screen reader experiences content in chronological order
responsive_behavior: Alternating layout collapses to single column on mobile
Horizontal timeline may become vertical on narrow screens
Content width adapts to container
Timestamps may move from beside to above content on mobile
common_mistakes: Not using semantic ordered list
Visual order doesn't match DOM order
Connector lines break on varying content heights
No empty state when timeline has no events
Timestamps in ambiguous format without locale handling

patterns.name: Tree View
patterns.description: Hierarchical data display with expandable/collapsible nodes for navigating nested structures like file systems, organization charts, or category trees.

variants.name: simple
variants.when_to_use: Plain expandable text hierarchy

variants.name: with_icons
variants.when_to_use: File/folder type icons for each node

variants.name: selectable
variants.when_to_use: Single or multi-select nodes

variants.name: checkbox
variants.when_to_use: Checkable nodes with parent/child selection

variants.name: lazy_loaded
variants.when_to_use: Children fetched on expand for large trees

accessibility_requirements: role='tree' on container, role='treeitem' on nodes
aria-expanded on parent nodes
Arrow key navigation (up/down between nodes, left/right to collapse/expand)
aria-level for depth indication
aria-selected for selectable trees
Home/End keys jump to first/last visible node
responsive_behavior: Horizontal scroll for deeply nested trees on narrow screens
Indentation may reduce on mobile
Touch-friendly expand/collapse targets
May offer breadcrumb-style drill-down as mobile alternative
common_mistakes: Missing tree ARIA roles and keyboard patterns
Arrow keys not implemented per WAI-ARIA tree pattern
No visual indentation guides for deep nesting
Expand/collapse not keyboard accessible
Loading state not communicated when fetching children

patterns.name: Accordion
patterns.description: Vertically stacked expandable sections where each header toggles the visibility of its associated content panel.

variants.name: single
variants.when_to_use: Only one section can be expanded at a time

variants.name: multi
variants.when_to_use: Multiple sections can be open simultaneously

variants.name: flush
variants.when_to_use: No outer border, sections divided only by lines

variants.name: nested
variants.when_to_use: Accordions within accordion sections

accessibility_requirements: Headers are button elements (or role='button')
aria-expanded on each header button
aria-controls linking header to panel id
Panel has role='region' with aria-labelledby pointing to header
Enter/Space toggles expansion
Optionally, Home/End navigate between accordion headers
responsive_behavior: Full-width on all viewports
Content panels adapt to container width
May replace tabs on mobile viewports
Expand icon rotates to indicate state
common_mistakes: Headers not keyboard focusable
Content hidden with display:none but still in tab order
No visual indication of expanded state
Using accordion for content that should all be visible
Expand/collapse animation ignoring prefers-reduced-motion

patterns.name: Description List
patterns.description: Key-value pair display for presenting labeled data such as metadata, specifications, or profile details in a structured format.

variants.name: vertical
variants.when_to_use: Term above description, stacked layout

variants.name: horizontal
variants.when_to_use: Term and description side by side

variants.name: striped
variants.when_to_use: Alternating background colors for readability

variants.name: inline
variants.when_to_use: Compact inline display for simple pairs

accessibility_requirements: Uses dl, dt, dd semantic HTML elements
Terms and descriptions are properly paired
Group headers can use heading elements within dt
Screen readers announce term-description relationships
responsive_behavior: Horizontal layout collapses to vertical on mobile
Term column width adapts or becomes full-width label
Long descriptions wrap naturally
common_mistakes: Not using dl/dt/dd semantic elements
Inconsistent alignment between terms and descriptions
Term column too narrow causing wrapping
Missing dividers or structure in long lists
No loading skeleton for async data
signals: Label: Value pairs in a list
Two-column layout with terms and descriptions

patterns.name: Code Block
patterns.description: Syntax-highlighted code display with optional line numbers, copy button, language indicator, and code highlighting.

variants.name: inline
variants.when_to_use: Short code snippet within paragraph text

variants.name: block
variants.when_to_use: Multi-line code with syntax highlighting

variants.name: with_filename
variants.when_to_use: Code shown with file path header

variants.name: diff
variants.when_to_use: Showing additions and deletions

variants.name: executable
variants.when_to_use: Run button for interactive code examples

accessibility_requirements: Uses pre and code semantic elements
Language announced via aria-label or visible label
Copy button has accessible label and announces success
Line numbers are decorative (not selectable for copy)
Scrollable area is keyboard accessible
Syntax highlighting not relying solely on color
responsive_behavior: Horizontal scroll for long lines
Word wrap option for narrow screens
Copy button remains visible within scroll context
Font size may reduce slightly on mobile
common_mistakes: No horizontal scroll causing layout break
Line numbers copied with code content
Syntax colors with insufficient contrast
No copy-to-clipboard functionality
Language not indicated anywhere

patterns.name: Data Chart
patterns.description: Visualization container for rendering data as bar charts, line charts, pie charts, and other graphical representations.

variants.name: bar
variants.when_to_use: Comparing quantities across categories

variants.name: line
variants.when_to_use: Showing trends over time

variants.name: pie
variants.when_to_use: Showing parts of a whole (few segments)

variants.name: area
variants.when_to_use: Volume or cumulative trends

variants.name: scatter
variants.when_to_use: Correlation between two variables

variants.name: combo
variants.when_to_use: Multiple chart types overlaid

accessibility_requirements: Provide data table alternative for all charts
aria-label with chart summary on container
Color not sole differentiator for data series (use patterns)
Keyboard navigable data points
Tooltip content accessible via keyboard focus
role='img' with aria-label for decorative charts
responsive_behavior: Chart resizes to container width
Legend may move from side to bottom on narrow screens
Horizontal bar chart may be preferred on mobile
Axis labels may rotate or reduce on small viewports
Touch-friendly tooltip activation
common_mistakes: No alternative text or data table for accessibility
Relying only on color for series differentiation
Charts not responsive to container size
Missing loading and empty states
Axis truncation hiding important values
Y-axis not starting at zero for bar charts (misleading)

patterns.name: Alert
patterns.description: Inline notification banner conveying contextual feedback messages at info, success, warning, or error severity levels.

variants.name: standard
variants.when_to_use: Basic alert with icon and message

variants.name: with_title
variants.when_to_use: Alert with bold title and description

variants.name: with_actions
variants.when_to_use: Alert with action buttons or links

variants.name: dismissible
variants.when_to_use: User can close the alert

variants.name: accent_border
variants.when_to_use: Left border accent for subtler appearance

accessibility_requirements: role='alert' for important messages that need immediate attention
role='status' for informational messages
aria-live='polite' for non-urgent updates
Close button has aria-label='Dismiss alert'
Icon has aria-hidden='true' when text conveys the same meaning
Not using color alone to convey severity (icon + text required)
responsive_behavior: Full-width within content area
Text wraps naturally on narrow screens
Close button remains accessible and visible
Action links may stack below message on mobile
common_mistakes: Using role='alert' for non-urgent information (causes interruption)
Alert disappearing too quickly for users to read
Color as sole severity indicator
No close mechanism for persistent alerts
Stacking too many alerts overwhelming the user

patterns.name: Toast
patterns.description: Temporary floating notification that appears briefly to confirm an action or provide transient feedback without interrupting workflow.

variants.name: simple
variants.when_to_use: Brief confirmation message

variants.name: with_action
variants.when_to_use: Includes undo or follow-up action

variants.name: persistent
variants.when_to_use: Stays until manually dismissed

variants.name: stacked
variants.when_to_use: Multiple toasts queued in a stack

accessibility_requirements: role='status' with aria-live='polite'
Auto-dismiss timing allows adequate reading time (minimum 5 seconds)
Action button is keyboard accessible before toast dismisses
Toast queue doesn't overwhelm screen readers
Pause auto-dismiss on hover or focus
Respect prefers-reduced-motion for entrance animation
responsive_behavior: Positioned at bottom-center or top-right depending on context
Full-width on mobile viewports
Stacking direction adjusts to position
Safe area inset respected on mobile
common_mistakes: Disappearing before user can read or interact
Using toast for critical errors (use alert or dialog instead)
No way to access toast content after dismissal
Too many simultaneous toasts
Action button not reachable by keyboard before auto-dismiss
signals: Small floating box at screen edge

patterns.name: Modal
patterns.description: Focused overlay dialog that requires user interaction before returning to the main content, used for important content or forms that need full attention.

variants.name: standard
variants.when_to_use: Default centered modal with header, body, footer

variants.name: scrollable
variants.when_to_use: Content exceeds viewport height

variants.name: fullscreen
variants.when_to_use: Complex flows needing maximum space

variants.name: nested
variants.when_to_use: Modal launched from within another modal (avoid if possible)

variants.name: alert_dialog
variants.when_to_use: Urgent confirmation requiring immediate response

accessibility_requirements: role='dialog' with aria-modal='true'
aria-labelledby pointing to title element
aria-describedby pointing to body content if applicable
Focus trapped within modal while open
Focus moves to modal (first focusable element or close button) on open
Escape key closes modal
Focus returns to trigger element on close
Background content inert (aria-hidden on main content)
responsive_behavior: Full-width with margins on mobile
May become full-screen sheet on very small viewports
Footer buttons stack vertically on mobile
Scrollable body, fixed header and footer
common_mistakes: Focus not trapped within modal
Focus not returned to trigger on close
Background content still interactive
Escape key doesn't close modal
Nested modals creating confusing UX
Modal used for content that should be a page

patterns.name: Dialog
patterns.description: Decision-point overlay presenting a question or confirmation to the user, typically with two or three action buttons (confirm, cancel, alternative).

variants.name: confirmation
variants.when_to_use: Confirm before proceeding with action

variants.name: destructive
variants.when_to_use: Warn before irreversible destructive action

variants.name: information
variants.when_to_use: Acknowledge information before continuing

variants.name: prompt
variants.when_to_use: Collect simple input within dialog

accessibility_requirements: role='alertdialog' for urgent confirmations
aria-labelledby on title, aria-describedby on message
Focus moves to the least destructive action button
Escape key triggers cancel/dismiss action
Focus trapped within dialog
Background content inert
responsive_behavior: Centered with margins on all viewports
Buttons may stack on very narrow screens
Dialog width constrained with max-width
Content scrolls within dialog if needed
common_mistakes: Destructive action as the visually prominent button
Ambiguous button labels ('Yes'/'No' instead of action verbs)
Using dialog for content that doesn't need interruption
No way to dismiss without choosing an action
Focus on destructive button by default

patterns.name: Progress Bar
patterns.description: Linear progress indicator showing completion status of a task or process as a horizontal bar that fills from left to right.

variants.name: determinate
variants.when_to_use: Known completion percentage

variants.name: indeterminate
variants.when_to_use: Unknown duration, shows activity

variants.name: with_label
variants.when_to_use: Percentage or step text displayed

variants.name: segmented
variants.when_to_use: Multi-step progress with distinct segments

variants.name: buffer
variants.when_to_use: Shows loaded buffer ahead of playback position

accessibility_requirements: role='progressbar' with aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow
aria-valuetext for human-readable progress description
aria-label describing what is progressing
Indeterminate uses aria-valuenow removal
Progress updates announced via aria-live at reasonable intervals
responsive_behavior: Full-width within container
Label may move from beside to above on narrow screens
Bar height remains consistent across viewports
common_mistakes: No accessible label for what is being measured
Progress jumping backwards confusing users
Indeterminate bar used when progress is known
Updates too frequent causing screen reader verbosity
No error or completion state

patterns.name: Progress Circle
patterns.description: Circular progress indicator showing completion as a ring that fills clockwise, often used for compact progress or loading states.

variants.name: determinate
variants.when_to_use: Known percentage displayed as arc fill

variants.name: indeterminate
variants.when_to_use: Spinning animation for unknown duration

variants.name: with_content
variants.when_to_use: Percentage or icon displayed in center

variants.name: semi_circle
variants.when_to_use: Gauge-style half-circle display

accessibility_requirements: role='progressbar' with aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow
aria-label describing what is progressing
Center text is decorative if same info is in aria attributes
SVG-based implementation needs role='img' with accessible name
responsive_behavior: Scales with defined size tokens
Center text scales proportionally
Remains circular at all sizes
common_mistakes: Too small to see progress clearly
Spinning animation ignoring prefers-reduced-motion
No text alternative for the visual progress
Center content overlapping ring on small sizes
Using circle for long-running processes where bar is more informative

patterns.name: Skeleton
patterns.description: Loading placeholder that mimics the shape and layout of the content being loaded, reducing perceived wait time and preventing layout shift.

variants.name: text
variants.when_to_use: Placeholder for text paragraphs

variants.name: circular
variants.when_to_use: Placeholder for avatars or icons

variants.name: rectangular
variants.when_to_use: Placeholder for images or cards

variants.name: custom
variants.when_to_use: Matches exact layout of target component

accessibility_requirements: aria-busy='true' on skeleton container
aria-label describing loading state
Screen reader announces when loading completes
Animation respects prefers-reduced-motion
Not used as the only loading indicator (announce loading state)
responsive_behavior: Matches the responsive behavior of the content it represents
Skeleton widths adapt to container
Number of skeleton items may reduce on mobile
common_mistakes: Skeleton layout doesn't match actual content layout
No accessible loading announcement
Skeleton visible for too brief a time causing flash
Not animating (static gray boxes look broken)
Using skeleton for actions that take under 300ms
signals: Gray animated shapes matching content layout
Replaced by real content on load

patterns.name: Spinner
patterns.description: Indeterminate loading indicator showing that a process is in progress without known completion time, typically a rotating circular animation.

variants.name: inline
variants.when_to_use: Small spinner within buttons or inputs

variants.name: overlay
variants.when_to_use: Centered over content area with backdrop

variants.name: page
variants.when_to_use: Full-page loading state

variants.name: with_label
variants.when_to_use: Spinner with descriptive loading text

accessibility_requirements: role='status' with aria-live='polite'
aria-label describing what is loading (e.g., 'Loading search results')
Visible label preferred over aria-label alone
Screen reader announces loading start and completion
Animation respects prefers-reduced-motion
responsive_behavior: Size scales across predefined tokens
Centered within its context area
Label may wrap below spinner on narrow screens
common_mistakes: No accessible label for what is loading
Spinner too small to notice
Used when progress percentage is known (use progress bar)
No completion announcement for screen readers
Animation continues after content has loaded
signals: Partial ring or dots in circle

patterns.name: Empty State
patterns.description: Placeholder content shown when a view or container has no data to display, guiding users on what to do next.

variants.name: no_data
variants.when_to_use: Collection exists but has no items yet

variants.name: no_results
variants.when_to_use: Search or filter returned no matches

variants.name: error
variants.when_to_use: Data failed to load

variants.name: first_use
variants.when_to_use: Feature not yet configured, onboarding moment

variants.name: permission
variants.when_to_use: User lacks access to this content

accessibility_requirements: Illustration has role='img' with alt text or aria-hidden if decorative
Title and description are semantic heading and paragraph
Action buttons are clearly labeled
State announced to screen readers when view transitions to empty
responsive_behavior: Centered within container
Illustration may scale down or hide on mobile
Text centered with max-width constraint
Actions stack vertically on narrow screens
common_mistakes: No guidance on what to do next
Generic message without context ('No data')
Missing illustration making the state look broken
Action button label doesn't match the empty state context
Empty state not occupying enough visual space

patterns.name: Error Boundary
patterns.description: Error recovery display that catches component-level failures and presents a graceful fallback UI with retry or recovery options.

variants.name: inline
variants.when_to_use: Error within a section, rest of page works

variants.name: page
variants.when_to_use: Full page error recovery

variants.name: minimal
variants.when_to_use: Compact error indicator with retry

variants.name: with_details
variants.when_to_use: Expandable error details for debugging

accessibility_requirements: Error state announced via aria-live='assertive'
Retry button clearly labeled and keyboard accessible
Error details accessible but not overwhelming
Focus moves to error boundary when error occurs
Report link opens in new context with warning
responsive_behavior: Fills the space of the failed component
Error message centered within boundary
Actions stack on narrow screens
Details collapsible to save space
common_mistakes: Showing raw error stack trace to users
No retry mechanism
Error boundary too granular or too broad
Not logging errors for debugging
Retry causes same error in a loop without backoff

patterns.name: Banner
patterns.description: Persistent message bar at the top of the page or section for site-wide announcements, system status, or cookie consent notices.

variants.name: announcement
variants.when_to_use: Marketing or feature announcement

variants.name: system_status
variants.when_to_use: System maintenance or outage notice

variants.name: consent
variants.when_to_use: Cookie or privacy consent banner

variants.name: upgrade
variants.when_to_use: Plan upgrade or trial expiration notice

accessibility_requirements: role='banner' or role='complementary' depending on purpose
Not role='alert' unless truly urgent (avoid interrupting)
Close button has descriptive aria-label
Banner content readable by screen readers in page flow
Does not push content below in a jarring way
responsive_behavior: Full-width across viewport
Text wraps, actions may stack on mobile
Fixed position or scrolls with content
May reduce to single-line with expand trigger on mobile
common_mistakes: Banner covers important UI elements
No close/dismiss mechanism for non-essential banners
Too many banners stacking at top of page
Reappearing after user has dismissed
Using banner for errors that should be inline alerts
signals: Full-width bar at top of page

patterns.name: Container
patterns.description: Content width constraint wrapper that centers content and applies a maximum width, providing consistent horizontal margins across the layout.

variants.name: fixed
variants.when_to_use: Fixed max-width at each breakpoint

variants.name: fluid
variants.when_to_use: Full-width with consistent padding

variants.name: narrow
variants.when_to_use: Reading-optimized narrow content width

variants.name: wide
variants.when_to_use: Wide content for dashboards or data views

accessibility_requirements: Semantic main, section, or article element
Content readable at all container widths
No horizontal scroll caused by content overflow
responsive_behavior: Max-width changes at breakpoints
Horizontal padding adjusts for mobile vs desktop
Fluid variant fills viewport with consistent padding
common_mistakes: Nesting containers causing double padding
Inconsistent container widths across pages
Content overflowing container on mobile
Using container for full-bleed backgrounds (need wrapper outside)

patterns.name: Grid
patterns.description: Column-based layout system dividing content into a configurable number of equal or variable-width columns with consistent gutters.

variants.name: fixed_columns
variants.when_to_use: Standard 12-column layout grid

variants.name: auto_fit
variants.when_to_use: Columns auto-fill based on minimum width

variants.name: asymmetric
variants.when_to_use: Unequal column widths (2/3 + 1/3)

variants.name: masonry
variants.when_to_use: Variable-height items filling vertical gaps

accessibility_requirements: Visual grid order matches DOM reading order
Grid is presentational, not table semantics
Content remains meaningful when linearized on mobile
Focus order follows logical content flow, not visual position
responsive_behavior: Column count reduces at smaller breakpoints
12 columns on desktop, 6 on tablet, 1 on mobile typical
Gutter size may reduce on mobile
Items may reorder based on priority
common_mistakes: Visual order doesn't match focus/DOM order
Grid items overflowing gutters
Inconsistent gutter sizes within the same grid
Not testing grid collapse on mobile
Using CSS grid order creating confusing tab sequences
signals: Items aligned in rows and columns

patterns.name: Stack
patterns.description: Flexbox-based layout utility for arranging child elements vertically (VStack) or horizontally (HStack) with consistent spacing.

variants.name: vertical
variants.when_to_use: Stack items top to bottom

variants.name: horizontal
variants.when_to_use: Stack items left to right

variants.name: responsive
variants.when_to_use: Horizontal on desktop, vertical on mobile

variants.name: with_dividers
variants.when_to_use: Visual dividers between stacked items

accessibility_requirements: DOM order matches visual order
Spacing is visual only, not affecting content semantics
Use appropriate semantic wrapper (div, section, etc.)
responsive_behavior: Direction may switch at breakpoints
Spacing may adjust at breakpoints
Wrapping behavior for horizontal stacks
Items may change alignment on different viewports
common_mistakes: Inconsistent spacing values within same stack
Using margins on children instead of gap on container
Not handling wrapping for horizontal stacks
Flex direction not responsive when it should be

patterns.name: Divider
patterns.description: Visual separator line between content sections, available in horizontal and vertical orientations with optional label.

variants.name: horizontal
variants.when_to_use: Separate stacked content sections

variants.name: vertical
variants.when_to_use: Separate side-by-side elements

variants.name: with_label
variants.when_to_use: Divider with centered text like 'OR'

variants.name: inset
variants.when_to_use: Indented divider that doesn't span full width

accessibility_requirements: role='separator' for semantic meaning
aria-orientation='horizontal' or 'vertical'
Decorative dividers use aria-hidden='true'
Label text should be in the accessibility tree if meaningful
responsive_behavior: Horizontal dividers span container width
Vertical dividers span container height
Orientation may change between viewports if layout changes
common_mistakes: Using hr without considering its semantic weight
Excessive dividers reducing whitespace effectiveness
Divider color too prominent or too subtle
Vertical dividers not stretching to container height

patterns.name: Aspect Ratio
patterns.description: Container that maintains a fixed width-to-height proportion regardless of content or viewport size, commonly used for images and video embeds.

variants.name: square
variants.when_to_use: 1:1 ratio for thumbnails and avatars

variants.name: video
variants.when_to_use: 16:9 ratio for video embeds

variants.name: photo
variants.when_to_use: 4:3 or 3:2 ratio for photography

variants.name: custom
variants.when_to_use: Any custom ratio specified

accessibility_requirements: Content within must remain accessible at all aspect ratios
Images have alt text regardless of container
No content clipping that hides important information
aspect-ratio CSS property preferred over padding-top hack
responsive_behavior: Width fills container, height calculated from ratio
Content scales or crops within the ratio container
Object-fit controls how content fills the space
common_mistakes: Content distorted due to incorrect object-fit
Important content cropped at certain ratios
Using padding-top hack when aspect-ratio CSS is available
Not handling content that doesn't match the expected ratio

patterns.name: Responsive Columns
patterns.description: Adaptive column layout that automatically adjusts the number of columns based on viewport width and minimum column width constraints.

variants.name: auto_fit
variants.when_to_use: Columns fill available space, wrapping as needed

variants.name: auto_fill
variants.when_to_use: Columns maintain min-width, leaving gaps if needed

variants.name: breakpoint
variants.when_to_use: Column count set explicitly at each breakpoint

variants.name: sidebar_main
variants.when_to_use: One fixed-width sidebar with fluid main content

accessibility_requirements: Content order in DOM matches reading order
No content hidden by column overflow
Content readable when linearized to single column
responsive_behavior: Columns reflow automatically based on container width
Minimum column width prevents content from becoming too narrow
Gutters may adjust at breakpoints
Single column on mobile is the natural fallback
common_mistakes: Columns too narrow causing unreadable content
Content reordering creating confusing reading flow
Not testing intermediate viewport sizes
Fixed column counts that don't adapt
signals: Content in multiple columns that reflows
auto-fit or auto-fill in CSS grid

patterns.name: Sidebar Layout
patterns.description: Two-panel layout with a fixed or resizable sidebar alongside a fluid main content area, common in applications and documentation sites.

variants.name: left_sidebar
variants.when_to_use: Navigation or tooling sidebar on the left

variants.name: right_sidebar
variants.when_to_use: Details or properties panel on the right

variants.name: dual_sidebar
variants.when_to_use: Both left and right sidebars

variants.name: resizable
variants.when_to_use: User can drag to resize sidebar width

accessibility_requirements: Sidebar and main content are distinct landmarks
Toggle button has aria-expanded state
Resize handle is keyboard operable
Content in sidebar remains in focus order when visible
Sidebar overlay traps focus when active on mobile
responsive_behavior: Sidebar collapses or becomes overlay on mobile
Main content takes full width when sidebar is hidden
Toggle control appears at mobile breakpoint
Sidebar width may reduce on tablet
common_mistakes: Main content not expanding when sidebar collapses
Sidebar overlay not dismissible on mobile
Resize handle not keyboard accessible
Content in collapsed sidebar still receives focus
No smooth transition between states
signals: One narrow panel beside wide content

patterns.name: Drawer
patterns.description: Panel that slides in from a screen edge (left, right, top, or bottom) to display secondary content, navigation, or forms without leaving the current context.

variants.name: left
variants.when_to_use: Navigation drawer from left edge

variants.name: right
variants.when_to_use: Detail panel or form from right edge

variants.name: top
variants.when_to_use: Notifications or search from top edge

variants.name: bottom
variants.when_to_use: Mobile-friendly bottom sheet alternative

variants.name: persistent
variants.when_to_use: Always-visible side panel without backdrop

accessibility_requirements: role='dialog' with aria-modal='true' for modal drawers
aria-labelledby pointing to drawer title
Focus trapped within drawer while open
Escape key closes drawer
Focus returns to trigger on close
Background content inert when drawer is modal
responsive_behavior: May go full-width on mobile
Persistent drawers become overlay on mobile
Swipe gesture to close on touch devices
Bottom drawer uses sheet-like behavior on mobile
common_mistakes: Drawer pushes content off-screen instead of overlaying
Focus not trapped in modal drawer
No backdrop for modal drawers
Drawer content not scrollable
Closing animation janky or missing

patterns.name: Dropdown Menu
patterns.description: Floating action menu that appears when triggered by a button click, displaying a list of actions or options.

variants.name: simple
variants.when_to_use: Flat list of action items

variants.name: with_icons
variants.when_to_use: Actions paired with recognizable icons

variants.name: with_shortcuts
variants.when_to_use: Keyboard shortcut hints shown per item

variants.name: nested
variants.when_to_use: Sub-menus for categorized actions

variants.name: with_sections
variants.when_to_use: Grouped actions with section headers

accessibility_requirements: Trigger has aria-haspopup='menu' and aria-expanded
role='menu' on container, role='menuitem' on items
Arrow key navigation between items
Enter/Space activates focused item
Escape closes menu and returns focus to trigger
Type-ahead to jump to items by first letter
Sub-menus open with right arrow, close with left arrow
responsive_behavior: Menu repositions to stay within viewport
Touch-friendly item sizing on mobile
May become full-width bottom sheet on mobile
Sub-menus may inline-expand instead of flying out
common_mistakes: Not using menu ARIA roles (using listbox instead)
Tab key moves between items instead of arrow keys
Menu opens on hover without click trigger
No type-ahead functionality for long menus
Menu renders outside viewport
signals: Items with optional icons and shortcuts

patterns.name: Context Menu
patterns.description: Right-click triggered action menu appearing at the cursor position, providing contextual actions relevant to the clicked element.

variants.name: simple
variants.when_to_use: Basic right-click menu with actions

variants.name: with_sub_menus
variants.when_to_use: Nested action categories

variants.name: contextual
variants.when_to_use: Different items based on clicked element type

variants.name: with_preview
variants.when_to_use: Action items with result preview

accessibility_requirements: Menu accessible via keyboard alternative (Shift+F10 or Menu key)
role='menu' with role='menuitem' items
Arrow key navigation
Escape closes menu, focus returns to triggering context
Alternative access to all actions via visible UI elements
Not the sole way to access important actions
responsive_behavior: Positioned at cursor/touch point
Repositions to stay within viewport bounds
May not apply on touch devices (use long-press or action menu instead)
Actions available through other UI paths on mobile
common_mistakes: Context menu as only access point for actions
No keyboard alternative to right-click
Menu extends outside viewport
Overriding browser context menu without good reason
Not providing same actions in visible UI

patterns.name: Sheet
patterns.description: Mobile-first bottom sheet overlay that slides up from the bottom of the screen, providing actions, content, or navigation with drag-to-dismiss gesture support.

variants.name: action
variants.when_to_use: List of action items (mobile alternative to dropdown)

variants.name: content
variants.when_to_use: Scrollable content panel from bottom

variants.name: modal
variants.when_to_use: Requires interaction before dismissal

variants.name: persistent
variants.when_to_use: Map-style sheet that stays visible at a peek height

accessibility_requirements: role='dialog' with aria-modal for modal sheets
Drag handle has aria-label and keyboard operability
Content accessible via keyboard scrolling
Escape or swipe down to dismiss
Focus trapped within modal sheets
Snap points communicated or intuitive
responsive_behavior: Primary pattern on mobile, may become drawer or modal on desktop
Respects safe area insets on notched devices
Snap points at percentage heights of viewport
Content scrolls within sheet when at max snap point
common_mistakes: No drag handle making sheet look like fixed content
Sheet content not scrollable at full height
Backdrop interaction not properly handled
Missing snap points causing awkward half-states
No keyboard alternative for drag gesture

patterns.name: Button
patterns.description: Primary interactive element for triggering actions, submitting forms, or navigating, available in multiple emphasis levels and sizes.

variants.name: primary
variants.when_to_use: Main call-to-action on the page or section

variants.name: secondary
variants.when_to_use: Supporting actions alongside primary

variants.name: tertiary
variants.when_to_use: Low-emphasis actions or text-style buttons

variants.name: destructive
variants.when_to_use: Delete, remove, or other irreversible actions

variants.name: outline
variants.when_to_use: Medium emphasis with border, no fill

variants.name: ghost
variants.when_to_use: Minimal emphasis, text-only appearance

accessibility_requirements: Semantic button element (not div or span with click handler)
Descriptive text label (not just 'Click here' or 'Submit')
Disabled state uses disabled attribute, not just visual styling
Loading state communicated via aria-busy='true' and aria-disabled
Focus indicator visible and high contrast
Minimum touch target 44x44 points
responsive_behavior: May go full-width on mobile viewports
Text truncation or wrapping for long labels
Icon-only with tooltip on very narrow screens (last resort)
